title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: When the bug moves, the system's angular momentum is conserved. This means that the initial angular momentum of the system (disk + bug) is equal to the final angular momentum.

title: Initial Angular Momentum
text: Calculate the initial angular momentum of the system when the bug is at the edge of the disk. Use the formula: \( $$L_i$$ $$=$$ I_{disk} \cdot \omega_i + m_{bug} \cdot $$R^2$$ \cdot \omega_i \) where \( I_{disk} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} \cdot M \cdot $$R^2$$ \).

title: Final Angular Momentum
text: When the bug is back at the edge, the final angular momentum \( $$L_f$$ \) is given by: \( $$L_f$$ $$=$$ I_{disk} \cdot \omega_f + m_{bug} \cdot $$R^2$$ \cdot \omega_f \).

title: Equating Initial and Final Angular Momentum
text: Since angular momentum is conserved, set \( $$L_i$$ $$=$$ $$L_f$$ \). Solve for \( \omega_f \) to find the final angular velocity when the bug is back at the edge.

title: Calculate \( \omega_f \)
text: Using the conservation of angular momentum, solve for \( \omega_f \): \( \omega_f $$=$$ \frac{I_{disk} \cdot \omega_i + m_{bug} \cdot $$R^2$$ \cdot \omega_i}{I_{disk} + m_{bug} \cdot $$R^2$$} \).
